strongest_current_evidence: Defesa Civil records 2021-2022; Sentinel-2 public imagery available; CPRM reports accessible; PE3D municipal layers available for review
strongest_missing_evidence: Independent annotated high-resolution image post-event; formal spatial data access from Defesa Civil; field validation observation
next_required_action: 1. Request formal access to COMPDEC spatial data and event maps (2021, 2022 events); 2. Identify and contact specialized annotation source for Sentinel-2 post-event validation; 3. Acquire Sentinel-2 L2A imagery 2021-05 to 2021-07 and 2022-03 to 2022-05; 4. Download GFM/CEMS products for same periods; 5. Schedule preliminary human review meeting with CPRM/INPE specialist
allowed_claim: Events in 2021 and 2022 are documented by Defesa Civil; Sentinel-2 imagery is publicly available for those periods; Copernicus products cover the region; radiometric change is observable on Sentinel-2; no operational ground truth can be declared without triangulation of multiple independent sources and human expert review.
forbidden_claim: Operational ground truth; automated flood detection; flood prediction; supervised training labels; sole reliance on algorithmic products (GFM, DINO); any claim that a single source (satellite, DINO, product) definitively identifies inundation extent.
notes: Recife priority: HIGH. Urban area, major metros, good Sentinel coverage, accessible official records. Blocker: need formal spatial access from Defesa Civil and independent annotation. DINO review-only for consistency check, never as evidence. GFM is context, not ground truth. Timeline to MEDIUM readiness: 3-4 months if data access approved.

strongest_current_evidence: Defesa Civil Feb 2022 records (high-profile event); CPRM post-evento report available; Sentinel-2 peri-event imagery accessible; national media coverage confirms event
strongest_missing_evidence: Independent observation/validation of slope failure and valley flooding extent; field survey by geomorphologist; high-resolution annotated post-event image
next_required_action: 1. Retrieve CPRM Feb 2022 official post-evento geological assessment report and maps; 2. Contact Defesa Civil Petropolis for structured spatial event documentation; 3. Access Sentinel-2 pre- and post-event imagery Feb 2022 (check cloud cover); 4. Review meteorological data (UFRJ) for Feb 2022 precipitation; 5. Schedule human review with CPRM geomorphologist and INPE flood specialist; 6. Plan field validation (if access feasible).
allowed_claim: Feb 2022 event is nationally documented and confirmed; Defesa Civil registered affected areas; CPRM published geological analysis; Sentinel-2 imagery captures pre-/post-event state; event correlation with extreme precipitation documented; no operational ground truth can be declared without integration and expert human review.
forbidden_claim: Operational ground truth; automated flood/landslide detection; prediction of future events in similar conditions; supervised training labels; any claim that satellite data alone defines landslide or flood extent without geomorphological and field validation.
notes: Petropolis priority: HIGH (extreme event, national significance, good record coverage). Complexity: landslide and flood combination; SAR/optical interpretation challenging on slopes. Strength: CPRM geological expertise available. Blocker: field access and geomorphological expert time. Timeline to MEDIUM readiness: 4-6 months if CPRM and field resources available.

strongest_current_evidence: GeoCuritiba excellent baseline (rede pluvial, drenagem, topografia); Defesa Civil 2022-2023 event records; Sentinel-2 urban coverage good; GFM/CEMS coverage available
strongest_missing_evidence: Defesa Civil structured 2022-2023 event spatial data; independent annotation of Sentinel-2 urban alagamento; municipal drainage operation records (valve activation history); field ground-truthing
next_required_action: 1. Contact Defesa Civil Curitiba for 2022-2023 event spatial documentation and structured data; 2. Access GeoCuritiba portal and download rede_pluvial, drenagem, topografia layers (document all CRS and metadata); 3. Acquire Sentinel-2 L2A imagery 2022-2023 event periods; 4. Request municipal drainage operation logs (valve activation, pump records) if available; 5. Identify annotation source for Sentinel-2 urban flooding validation; 6. Schedule human review with municipal engineer and INPE specialist.
allowed_claim: Curitiba 2022-2023 events documented by Defesa Civil; urban alagamento (not landslide) is primary hazard; Sentinel-2 10 m resolution appropriate for urban areas; GeoCuritiba provides excellent baseline spatial context (rede pluvial, drenagem); no operational ground truth can be declared without formal data access and expert human review.
forbidden_claim: Operational ground truth; automated flood detection algorithms; flood prediction models; supervised training labels; sole reliance on GFM or satellite products; any claim that drainage/topographic models predict specific event occurrence.
notes: Curitiba priority: HIGH (systematic urban alagamento, excellent baseline data, well-organized municipal GIS). Strength: GeoCuritiba is gold standard for baseline. Complexity: urban context (buildings, concrete) may affect Sentinel-2 interpretation. Blocker: formal data access from Defesa Civil. Timeline to MEDIUM readiness: 3-5 months if municipal cooperation obtained.
